paper: Li et al., A Consistency-Improved LiDAR(-Inertial) Bundle Adjustment, arXiv:2602.06380
claim: On IMU-free KITTI odometry, sliding-window plane BA improves whole-run ATE slightly but worsens translational RPE and throughput versus the committed no-BA profile.

protocol.seed: first-pose anchor, --no-gt-seed velocity prediction after frame 0

variants.ba_on: sliding-window plane BA with FEJ enabled (default enable_ba=1)

conclusion: Under IMU-free KITTI full-sequence odometry with constant-velocity prediction, enabling sliding-window plane BA worsens translational RPE by about 1.4–1.9 percentage points while lowering ATE by roughly 3–14% and cutting throughput by about 24–29%. The frozen bundle therefore keeps the no-BA profile as the main KITTI row; BA remains implemented and unit-tested but is not the default public claim on this protocol.
